summary.intent: Implement the ScrollArea agnostic core component (issue #279), covering state management, axis metrics, thumb drag, track click, hover/visibility logic, auto-hide timer, and orientation gating, with full tests and spec synchronance.
summary.outcome: ScrollArea agnostic core fully implemented, audited, and pushed via PR #699 with two rounds of Codex review fixes applied (9 total defects resolved across P2 threads), 53 passing tests including snapshot fixtures, proptest, and conformance harnesses, and zero clippy/fmt warnings.

repo: Timer/delay effects follow a named-intent pattern (Effect enum owned by adapters); the agnostic core never calls set_timeout or use_platform_effects directly — canonical reference: tooltip and rating_group components.
Delay-with-a-default fields use plain std::time::Duration, never a u32 of milliseconds or Option<Duration>.
context_changed is set whenever an apply closure runs, not on value-diff — tests must not assert context_changed=false after no-op events.
Snapshot acceptance can be blocked by unrelated i18n-gated snapshots unreferenced without the feature flag; scroll_area snapshots must be promoted independently.
The pre-existing pagination/mod.rs map_or_else clippy error is a floating-stable vs. pinned-toolchain divergence and must be fixed before CI unblocks on Rust 1.96.0.
Post-implementation audit is a mandatory phase: Phase 1 (spec/impl drift), Phase 2 (adapter spec completeness), Phase 3 (coverage ≥ target) are all required before final verification.

code.finding: Vertical-only ScrollArea must never set scrollbar_x_visible; orientation gating belongs in core via ScrollOrientation::allows_x/allows_y, not left as adapter concern.
on_props_changed was not implemented initially; controlled prop changes never synced into Context until SyncProps event + on_props_changed were added.
ThumbDragMove new_thumb_pos was clamped at 0 but not at track_size − thumb_size, allowing over-scroll via drag.
Hover could get stuck visible after leaving an overlaid track; fixed by adding hovering_root to Context and hiding on last leave (root or scrollbar).
ThumbDragEnd in Scroll mode never re-emitted the AutoHide effect, leaving scrollbars permanently visible after a drag.
Resize didn't clear stale visibility in Hover/Scroll modes; update_visibility must clamp flags off for non-overflowing axes in all modes.
Pre-existing map_or_else lint (should be unwrap_or_else) blocks workspace clippy on Rust 1.96.0; fixed as zero-risk 1-line change to unblock CI.

workflow: Run cargo xci-fast (all 10 gates) as the mandatory pre-push gate; it covers clippy, fmt, tests, snapshots, doc build, and orphan check.
Accept new insta snapshots separately from the main test run to avoid being blocked by unrelated feature-gated snapshot failures.
Codex review triggered by posting @codex in the PR; two rounds were needed — each resolves a batch of P2 threads before re-triggering.
TDD discipline for Codex-review fixes: write a failing test reproducing the exact defect, apply the fix, verify the test passes — prevents regression and documents the contract.
Proptest seeds from earlier (since-removed) invariant failures must be reverted to avoid false-positive failures on committed code.
Background poll tasks for external review systems should be explicitly cancelled when the reviewer is unavailable, rather than left running.

friction: context_only closures required explicit &mut Context annotations — took multiple edit iterations across ThumbDragMove, TrackClick, and other handlers before the borrow checker was satisfied.
Snapshot --accept was blocked by pre-existing i18n-gated highlight snapshots unreferenced without the feature flag, requiring manual promotion of scroll_area snapshots.
A background job race condition during snapshot cleanup required waiting and a full clean re-run.
Local floating stable clippy (1.96.0) flagged pagination/mod.rs which was pre-existing committed code, creating ambiguity that required confirmation before fixing.
A leftover proptest seed from a removed invariant produced a false-positive failure on committed code and had to be identified and reverted.
Messages struct needed PartialEq derived because Context derives it — not obvious from the spec, discovered only at compile time.
gh API thread replies failed silently with -f flag; required switching to -F for correct serialization.
open_items: Codex pass 2 re-trigger is pending; a third review round may produce additional threads requiring fixes.
The i18n-gated highlight snapshot issue that blocked initial --accept is pre-existing and unresolved — will continue affecting PRs touching snapshots without the i18n feature flag.
Merge and issue #279 close are pending Codex 👍 + green CI; not yet complete.
